title_en: Commercial, Family, and Criminal Law Sections
content_id:
Bagian-bagian dari As-Sunan al-Kubra yang membahas hukum dagang, hubungan keluarga, dan peradilan pidana mencerminkan luasnya cakupan al-Bayhaqi terhadap fikih Islam. Bagian-bagian dagang membahas persyaratan hukum untuk transaksi jual-beli yang sah, kondisi yang membatalkan kontrak, larangan riba dan ketidakpastian yang berlebihan, dan hukum-hukum khusus tentang berbagai jenis kesepakatan komersial seperti kemitraan, sewa, dan pembelian di muka. Al-Bayhaqi menyajikan bukti hadis untuk hukum-hukum ini dengan kombinasi khasnya antara penilaian autentikasi dan analisis hukum.

Bagian-bagian hukum keluarga mencakup seluruh rentang hukum domestik Islam: pernikahan dan syarat-syaratnya, hak dan kewajiban suami istri, berbagai bentuk cerai dan konsekuensinya, masa tunggu, hak asuh anak setelah perceraian, dan kewajiban finansial yang tetap ada setelah pembubaran pernikahan. Bagian-bagian ini dikembangkan secara luas dan merupakan penyajian yang komprehensif dari hukum keluarga Syafi'i yang berakar dalam bukti hadis yang terautentikasi. Kutipan terperinci dari para Sahabat dan fuqaha awal di samping tradisi-tradisi kenabian menjadikan bab-bab ini berharga bagi para ulama yang mengerjakan sejarah hukum keluarga Islam.

Bagian-bagian warisan menyajikan perhitungan kompleks dari hukum warisan Islam melalui bukti hadis yang mendasari setiap keputusan. Pekerjaan autentikasi al-Bayhaqi dalam bab-bab ini sangat penting karena hukum warisan bergantung pada sejumlah kecil tradisi kunci yang keandalannya menjadi fondasi seluruh sistem. Penilaian cermatnya terhadap sanad-sanad yang berkaitan dengan hukum warisan telah secara rutin dikutip oleh ulama-ulama berikutnya sebagai panduan otoritatif.

Bagian-bagian hukum pidana mencakup hukuman tetap untuk kejahatan serius, kondisi penerapannya, dan persyaratan pembuktian dalam prosedur pidana Islam. Presentasi al-Bayhaqi menekankan persyaratan yang ketat untuk membuktikan kesalahan dalam kasus-kasus yang melibatkan hukuman mati dan badan, mencerminkan pemahaman Syafi'i bahwa tradisi kenabian bermaksud agar hukuman-hukuman ini jarang diterapkan dalam praktik sambil tetap berlaku secara teoritis sebagai ungkapan hukum ilahi.
content_en:
The sections of As-Sunan al-Kubra dealing with commercial law, family relations, and criminal justice represent the full breadth of al-Bayhaqi's coverage of Islamic jurisprudence. The commercial sections address the legal requirements for valid sales, the conditions that invalidate contracts, the prohibition of usury and excessive uncertainty, and the specific rulings on various types of commercial arrangements such as partnership, leasing, and advance purchase. Al-Bayhaqi presents the hadith evidence for these rulings with his characteristic combination of authentication assessment and legal analysis.

The family law sections cover the full range of Islamic domestic law: marriage and its conditions, the rights and obligations of the spouses, the various forms of divorce and their consequences, the waiting period, custody of children after divorce, and the financial obligations that persist after dissolution of the marriage. These sections are extensively developed and represent a comprehensive presentation of Shafi'i family law grounded in authenticated hadith evidence. The detailed citations from the Companions and early jurists alongside the prophetic traditions make these chapters valuable for scholars working on the history of Islamic family law.

The inheritance sections present the complex calculations of Islamic inheritance law through the hadith evidence that underlies each ruling. Al-Bayhaqi's authentication work in these chapters is particularly important because inheritance law depends on a relatively small number of key traditions whose reliability is foundational to the entire system. His careful assessment of the chains bearing on inheritance rulings has been regularly cited by subsequent scholars as authoritative guidance.

The criminal law sections cover the fixed penalties for serious crimes, the conditions for their application, and the evidentiary requirements of Islamic criminal procedure. Al-Bayhaqi's presentation emphasizes the stringent requirements for establishing guilt in capital and corporal cases, reflecting the Shafi'i understanding that the prophetic tradition intended these penalties to be rarely applied in practice while remaining theoretically in force as expressions of divine law.
content_ar: The sections of As-Sunan al-Kubra dealing with commercial law, الأسرة relations, and criminal العدل represent the full breadth of al-Bayhaqi's coverage of Islamic jurisprudence. The commercial sections address the legal requirements for valid sales, the conditions that invalidate contracts, the prohibition of usury and excessive uncertainty, and the specific rulings on various types of commercial arrangements مثل partnership, leasing, and advance purchase. Al-Bayhaqi presents the الحديث evidence for these rulings with his characteristic combination of authentication assessment and legal analysis. The الأسرة law sections cover the full range of Islamic domestic law: marriage and its conditions, the الحقوق and obligations of the spouses, the various forms of divorce and their consequences, the waiting period, custody of الأولاد بعد divorce, and the financial obligations that persist بعد dissolution of the marriage. These sections are extensively developed and represent a comprehensive presentation of الشافعي الأسرة law grounded in authenticated الحديث evidence. The detailed citations from the الصحابة and early jurists alongside the prophetic traditions make these الأبواب valuable for العلماء working on the history of Islamic الأسرة law. The inheritance sections present the complex calculations of Islamic inheritance law through the الحديث evidence that underlies each ruling. Al-Bayhaqi's authentication work in these الأبواب is particularly المهم لأن inheritance law depends on a relatively small number of الرئيسي traditions whose reliability is foundational to the entire system. His careful assessment of the chains bearing on inheritance rulings has been regularly cited by subsequent العلماء as المعتمد الهداية. The criminal law sections cover the fixed penalties for serious crimes, the conditions for their application, and the evidentiary requirements of Islamic criminal procedure. Al-Bayhaqi's presentation emphasizes the stringent requirements for establishing guilt in capital and corporal cases, reflecting the الشافعي الفهم that the prophetic التراث intended these penalties to be rarely applied in practice بينما remaining theoretically in force as expressions of divine law.
